name: Technical Writing
description: A multiagent technical writing team for creating documentation, tutorials, and reference guides. Includes editor, writers, reviewer, and publisher with end-to-end content workflows.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Editor
agents.role: Editorial lead — manages content pipeline, assigns topics, reviews all content for quality and consistency, and maintains style guide

agents.name: Technical Writer
agents.role: Content creation — researches topics, writes documentation and tutorials, incorporates review feedback, and maintains technical accuracy

agents.name: Technical Reviewer
agents.role: Quality assurance — fact-checks technical content, tests code examples, verifies accuracy against source material, and suggests improvements

agents.name: Publisher
agents.role: Publishing operations — formats content for target platforms, manages publishing schedule, handles SEO metadata, and tracks content performance

communities.name: Writing Team
communities.description: All writing team coordination and announcements

groups.name: Review
groups.description: Content review, fact-checking, and revision tracking

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Content pipeline status and team coordination

workflows.name: Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the writing team and set editorial direction
workflows.content:
# Writing Team Kickoff

You are the Editor. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Topics to cover:** [e.g., API docs, getting started guide, architecture overview]
- **Target audience:** [e.g., developers, end users, internal team]
- **Style guide:** [Microsoft Style Guide, Google Developer Docs, Diátaxis, custom]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — leave blank if not using GitHub]
- **Output format:** [Markdown, MDX, reStructuredText, HTML, custom]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Writing Team community
2. Review the workspace for existing documentation or content plans
3. Create a content plan based on the topics above and assign to writers
4. Brief the reviewer on quality standards and fact-checking expectations
5. Set up the publishing calendar and post initial schedule to Status group
6. If a GitHub repo is configured, create issues for each writing assignment

workflows.name: Outline Review
workflows.description: Review and approve content outlines before writing begins
workflows.content:
# Outline Review

1. Writers: submit proposed outlines with target audience, scope, and key sections
2. Editor: review outline structure and alignment with content goals
3. Check for overlap with existing published content
4. Provide feedback on scope, depth, and approach
5. Approve outline and set deadline for first draft

workflows.name: Draft Writing
workflows.description: Daily writing check-in and draft progress tracking
workflows.content:
# Daily Draft Progress

1. Each writer: post progress update on assigned drafts
2. Flag any blockers (missing source material, unclear requirements)
3. For completed drafts: move to Review group for fact-checking
4. Editor: review pipeline and adjust priorities if needed
5. Post content pipeline status to Status group

workflows.name: Fact Check
workflows.description: Technical review and fact-checking of completed drafts
workflows.content:
# Fact Check & Technical Review

1. Read the draft thoroughly for technical accuracy
2. Test all code examples, commands, and API calls
3. Verify facts, statistics, and external references
4. Check for consistency with style guide and terminology
5. Submit review with corrections, suggestions, and approval status

workflows.name: Publish
workflows.content:
# Content Publishing

1. Take approved, reviewed content from the Review group
2. Format for the target platform (docs site, blog, wiki)
3. Add SEO metadata: title, description, keywords, open graph tags
4. Publish and verify the live page renders correctly
5. Post publication confirmation with link to Status group
